scores.brief_assessment: An excellent, well-structured reference with complete copy-ready templates, proprietary Grove API documentation (snippet annotations, Expect comparison library, ellipsis patterns), and concrete naming conventions — nearly every token directly supports creating a new Java Grove example, with only a small fraction covering standard Maven/JUnit knowledge an agent would already have.
scores.novel_info: The most novel details are the proprietary `mongodb.comparison.Expect` API — including its specific method signatures (`shouldMatch`, `shouldResemble`, `withSchema`, `withIgnoredFields`, `withOrderedSort`, `withUnorderedSort`), the schema validation keys (`count`, `required_fields`, `field_values`), the mutual exclusivity rules between `shouldResemble` and other methods, and the ellipsis pattern-matching syntax in expected output files (`"..."`, `["..."]`, `{ ... }`, standalone `...`) — none of which appear in any public MongoDB or JUnit documentation. The internal `snip.js` toolchain behavior (auto-running Spotless on output, the snippet filename format `YourExample.snippet.your-snippet-name.java`, and the output path `content/code-examples/tested/java/driver-sync/`) should also be fact-checked against the actual repository implementation.
